entries.note: feat-prepost-rendering is fully implemented. The reducer (src/web/src/store/reducer.ts) already had all three prepost.command.* handlers: prepost.command.started inserts a SystemNoticeBlock with runId, source=hook, pre/post label, and empty outputChunks[]; prepost.command.output appends chunks to the block via updateBlock (O(1) via _blockMap); prepost.command.ended finalizes with exitCode, action, prepostFinalized=true, severity update, and orphan detection (needsSnapshot). The SystemNoticeRenderer (src/web/src/components/LiveStream/SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx) renders the collapsible log region, stderr in text-red-400, and the exit code badge (green for 0, red for non-zero). WorkflowDetailRoute dispatches prepost.command.output via dispatchTextDelta (rAF-batched, satisfying AC-5/RC-2). This session added: (1) three prepost frame builders (prepostStartedFrame, prepostOutputFrame, prepostEndedFrame) to e2e/helpers.ts, (2) a __yokeGetSnapshot__ window test hook in main.tsx for AC-7 needsSnapshot assertion, and (3) src/web/e2e/prepost-rendering.spec.ts with 10 tests covering AC-1 through AC-8 (AC-5 is implicitly satisfied by the rAF path in WorkflowDetailRoute and is not separately tested with a timing assertion).
deferred_criteria: AC-5 rAF flush timing not explicitly asserted — the 16ms batching is wired in WorkflowDetailRoute (prepost.command.output → dispatchTextDelta) but the test suite does not contain a timing assertion measuring the batch interval. The behavior is correct but not directly asserted in e2e.
known_risks: AC-7 needsSnapshot test uses window.__yokeGetSnapshot__() which accesses the module-level render store directly. If the WS mock delivers the orphan ended frame before React has mounted and registered the frame handler, the assertion may race. The test waits for 'Session started' text before injecting the orphan frame to reduce this risk.

AC-6 follow-tail stability test relies on the pane overflow after 20 pre-filled blocks. If virtualizer estimateSize or container height changes, 20 blocks may not overflow and the test could produce a false pass (pill never appears regardless).

The AC-3 red-class check uses a CSS class substring match (text-red-) rather than a computed style check. If Tailwind purges or renames the class in a future version the test may fail without a logic regression.

entries.note: Fixed 4 test failures from attempt 1. (1) reducer.ts: removed the '— exit N' suffix that was appended to the prepost ended block's message. The exit code badge already displays the value; the duplicated text caused getByText('exit 0'/'exit 1') strict-mode violations in AC-4 and AC-8 tests. (2) WorkflowDetailRoute.tsx: routed all three prepost frame types (started, output, ended) through dispatchTextDelta instead of only output. This joins them in the same rAF flush queue as stream.text, preserving chronological order when a hook fires between text deltas (AC-8). (3) LiveStreamPane.tsx: added a totalSize-watching useEffect that re-scrolls to the last block when any block's measured height changes while follow-tail is active. This prevents the virtualizer's internal scroll correction from disengaging follow-tail when a prepost log is expanded or collapsed (AC-6). (4) prepost-rendering.spec.ts: tightened the AC-1 phase locator from /implement/ (matched both session.started and prepost notices) to /Pre-command: lint\.sh \(phase: implement\)/; changed AC-7 from page.evaluate (synchronous, before rAF) to page.waitForFunction (polls until needsSnapshot=true, tolerates rAF delay). All 10 prepost-rendering tests pass; full 162-test e2e suite passes with no regressions.
deferred_criteria: AC-5 rAF flush timing not explicitly asserted with a timing measurement. All three prepost frame types now route through dispatchTextDelta, satisfying the RC-2 requirement, but no e2e test measures the 16ms batch interval.
known_risks: The totalSize-based re-anchor effect in LiveStreamPane fires on every virtualizer measurement change. In a pane with many rapidly-updating blocks, this could trigger multiple scrollToIndex calls per rAF. The calls are idempotent but could conflict with a user who scrolls up while a height measurement is in flight.
AC-3 CSS class check still uses text-red- substring match rather than computed style. Tailwind version changes could invalidate the assertion without a logic regression.
AC-6 test still relies on 20 pre-filled blocks to overflow the pane for follow-tail to engage. If estimateSize or container height changes, the test may not overflow and could give a false pass.

blocking_issues:
check-verdict failed (exit 1):
Review verdict: FAIL — 1 blocking issue(s):
  • AC-4: prepost.command.ended must finalize the notice with both exit code AND action label. block.action is populated by the reducer (e.g. { type: 'continue' } or { type: 'abort' }) but SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx never renders it — only the 'exit N' badge is displayed. The e2e tests for AC-4 also do not assert the action label, so the gap is untested. A user cannot tell from the UI whether the hook resulted in continue vs abort.

entries.note: Fixed the sole blocking issue from attempt 2 review: AC-4 required the finalized prepost notice to display both exit code AND the action label (continue/abort). SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx now renders a second badge for block.action.type immediately after the exit code badge when prepostFinalized is true and action is a non-null object with a string type field. 'abort' gets a red-tinted badge; all other action types (e.g. 'continue') get a neutral gray badge. The AC-4 e2e tests were extended with assertions verifying the action label is visible after the ended frame arrives. All 10 prepost-rendering tests pass; full 162-test suite passes with no regressions.
deferred_criteria: AC-5 rAF flush timing not explicitly asserted with a timing measurement. All three prepost frame types route through dispatchTextDelta, satisfying the RC-2 requirement, but no e2e test measures the 16ms batch interval.
known_risks: The action badge type-guard uses 'block.action != null && typeof (block.action as Record<string,unknown>).type === string' — this covers { type: string } objects but will silently render nothing for action payloads with different shapes (e.g. action as a bare string or number). Acceptable for the current protocol but would need updating if the action shape evolves.
AC-3 CSS class check still uses text-red- substring match rather than computed style. Tailwind version changes could invalidate the assertion without a logic regression.
AC-6 test still relies on 20 pre-filled blocks to overflow the pane for follow-tail to engage. If estimateSize or container height changes, the test may not overflow and could give a false pass.

entries.note: No new code changes were made. The review-verdict.json found in the worktree root contains the attempt 2 verdict (AC-4: action label not rendered) but this issue was already fixed in attempt 3 (commit 147bca6: SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx renders block.action.type as a badge after the exit code badge). All 10 prepost-rendering e2e tests pass (AC-1 through AC-8). The test suite verifies both 'continue' and 'abort' action label visibility in the AC-4 tests. The stale review-verdict.json appears to be an artifact from the attempt 2 review that was not cleared before attempt 3's review ran, causing the harness to loop back unnecessarily. No deferred criteria remain beyond the previously acknowledged AC-5 timing assertion gap.
deferred_criteria: AC-5 rAF flush timing not explicitly asserted with a timing measurement. All three prepost frame types route through dispatchTextDelta (RC-2), but no e2e test measures the 16ms batch interval. The code path is correct.
known_risks: The review-verdict.json file in the worktree is stale (from attempt 2 review). If the harness reads it without re-running the reviewer, it will incorrectly report FAIL again. The reviewer should run the e2e test suite or inspect the current SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx (lines 60-71) to confirm the action label is rendered.
AC-3 CSS class check still uses text-red- substring match rather than computed style — fragile against Tailwind version changes.
AC-6 test relies on 20 pre-filled blocks to overflow the virtualizer container — could give a false pass if estimateSize or container height changes.

blocking_issues: AC-4: prepost.command.ended must finalize the notice with both exit code AND action label. block.action is populated by the reducer (e.g. { type: 'continue' } or { type: 'abort' }) but SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx never renders it — only the 'exit N' badge is displayed. The e2e tests for AC-4 also do not assert the action label, so the gap is untested. A user cannot tell from the UI whether the hook resulted in continue vs abort.
non_blocking: AC-5 rAF flush timing is not directly asserted in any e2e test (acknowledged in handoff; code path is correct).
AC-3 / AC-4 class checks use CSS class substring match (text-red-, green) rather than computed styles — fragile against Tailwind version changes.
AC-6 test relies on 20 pre-filled blocks to overflow the virtualizer container; if estimateSize or container height changes the overflow may not occur, risking a false pass.
